reasons.reason: Vertiv is expanding AI data-center cooling capacity with the June 12 ThermoKey acquisition and a July 1 Malaysia manufacturing expansion, both aimed at higher-density liquid-cooling and heat-rejection demand.
Vertiv sits in the AI infrastructure lane, where power and cooling spending is still attracting capital even as the broader tech trade gets more selective.

AI buildout is increasing demand for power and thermal management systems, which State Street highlights as part of the AI infrastructure barbell trade.
Vertiv was described as benefiting from AI cooling deals and raised guidance.

reasons.reason: Micron's July 9 announcement of up to $3 billion for the U.S. semiconductor ecosystem and its June Q3 results showing data-center revenue above a $25 billion annualized run rate confirm that HBM and DRAM are now strategic AI infrastructure, not just a commodity cycle.
Micron has pulled back with the semiconductor complex even though it is still one of the main AI-memory beneficiaries and analysts keep pointing to powerful earnings momentum.

Micron just dipped into a bear market, but the underlying AI memory cycle still looks intact because Reuters reporting in late June said HBM supply was effectively sold out for 2026 and the company remains one of the few scaled beneficiaries of AI compute bottlenecks in DRAM and HBM.
Benzinga’s July 8 alert showed a bullish MU call sweep on the Jul 13, 2026 $1,050 strike with 1,341 contracts and $38.7K of premium, which lines up with a strong AI-memory demand tape.

reasons.reason: ServiceNow's Now Assist is starting to show up in contract economics, with Q1 2026 customers spending over $1 million in annual contract value up more than 130% year over year and recent July coverage pointing to a $1.5 billion AI revenue target.
ServiceNow is still far below its highs, but Guggenheim just flipped to Buy on the view that the market is overpricing the AI threat to software.

ServiceNow's June 29 launch of AI-powered services with Accenture and its autonomous-workforce rollout show that enterprise AI is becoming an upsell layer inside the core workflow platform, which is exactly the kind of usage-based monetization that can reaccelerate SaaS growth.

reasons.reason: Apple's multiyear chip commitment worth up to $30 billion is a fresh revenue catalyst for Broadcom and reinforces its AI infrastructure role.

Apple's new $30 billion, through-2031 chip supply deal with Broadcom landed today just as Reuters coverage of Broadcom's latest results highlighted a sharply rising AI revenue outlook, making AVGO a current AI-semiconductor winner with both custom silicon demand and domestic manufacturing expansion visibility.

Apple's new $30B chip agreement with Broadcom is explicitly tied to expanding U.S. manufacturing and diversifying supply chains away from geopolitically sensitive Asian production, making AVGO a direct beneficiary of industrial-policy and nearshoring spending.
